candidate_results.description: A fixed UV algebra is progressively disclosed by a changing access window and fixed decoherence law.
candidate_results.reason: Every recorded mode is already in the fixed completed UV algebra; growth is aperture disclosure, not source-side issuance.

candidate_results.description: Each stage forms a UV mode label by diagonalizing the present stage context, modeling staged degree-of-freedom formation.
candidate_results.reason: The diagonal mode is fresh relative to each finite prefix context, but the realized whole family is absorbed by an after-the-fact singleton enumerator. This matches OnlineIssuance^LC, not a new physical surplus.

candidate_results.description: A modeler supplies a new stage law or hidden global table that declares future UV modes available.
candidate_results.reason: The new mode table or law is supplied from outside the stage-local source. It is an absorber/control, not a generated source witness.
